page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is configured with 96.0 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 90557.44 and a PassMark CPU score of 73075.26. The system excels in parallel processing tasks, as evidenced by high scores in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (76.47 GB/sec) and software compilation (49061.0 on Geekbench). Memory bandwidth is highly capable, reaching 4889.85 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 318.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1475.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1843.49.

The n2-highcpu-96 offers a specialized resource density optimized for compute-heavy workloads. By limiting memory allocation to 1.0 GB per vCPU and omitting local storage, the instance reduces overhead costs associated with unused RAM and storage capacity. This makes it a cost-efficient choice for raw processing tasks, though it presents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring high local disk I/O.

This server is highly suited for compute-bound workloads such as multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput static web serving. It is also capable of handling database operations and high-rate Redis transactions, as well as initial prompt processing for smaller large language models, provided the model size fits within the 96.0 GB memory limit.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and does not include local storage or GPUs. The server demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 73075.26 and high multi-threaded decompression and software compilation capabilities. Conversely, single-core performance is average. This configuration offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy tasks by minimizing memory overhead. It is best suited for parallel processing workloads, including software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and Redis operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads without local storage or GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core, compute-optimized virtual server designed for parallel processing, multi-threaded compilation, and high-throughput workloads without memory overhead.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.6 GHz
96.0 GB of dedicated system memory (1.0 GB per vCPU)
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for parallel processing, software compilation, and web serving
